content:
In 1997, Dharmendra Pradhan protested against paper leaks.

Protests are ongoing across the country over the NEET-UG exam, and demands for the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan have intensified.

Meanwhile, an incident from 1997 has resurfaced on social media.

Back then, around 1,500 students gathered outside the Odisha Secretariat in Bhubaneswar to protest an alleged exam paper leak. Dharmendra Pradhan, then National Secretary of ABVP, was part of the demonstration. The protest turned violent after a police lathi charge, and Pradhan was reportedly injured.

Today, people on social media are pointing out the irony: the student leader who once protested against paper leaks is now the Education Minister facing criticism over the NEET-UG controversy.

What do you think about this? Share your opinion in the comments.
comment: The larger issue is not just who protested or who is accountable today, but whether systems have strong safeguards that prevent paper leaks regardless of political leadership.
